data_schema: id(int row id, keep verbatim for submission); molecule_name(str, groups atom pairs of one molecule — group key); atom_index_0/atom_index_1(int indices of the coupled atom pair); type(categorical coupling type e.g. 1JHC,2JHH,3JHN — 8 distinct); scalar_coupling_constant(float target, Hz, train only). Extra files (structures.csv with per-atom x,y,z) exist in the data dir and can be joined on molecule_name+atom_index for geometry features.
extra_notes: Kaggle CHAMPS Molecular Scalar Coupling. Regression predicting NMR scalar coupling constant per atom pair. train=4.66M rows. LOCAL CV_SCORE = MAE (LOWER better). NOTE official metric = mean over the 8 coupling types of log(MAE_type); compute that as a secondary number but report cv_score as plain overall MAE for a stable proxy. Group-aware CV by molecule_name (GroupKFold) so no molecule leaks. Best features come from JOINING structures.csv to get 3D coordinates then computing interatomic DISTANCE between atom_index_0 and atom_index_1, plus one-hot of type. lightgbm/xgboost; subsample molecules for CV if needed but predict all test rows. submission.csv columns EXACTLY id,scalar_coupling_constant for all 2505190 test rows in order. metrics.json {"cv_score": <mae float>, "metric": "mae"}.
